title: Garden Party

jokes.text: If there's an opportunity for a graffiti artist to work in a... Phallic shape, interacting with the artwork, it'll happen. And Andy... Gave them that opportunity.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about the defaced billboard
jokes.punchline: Andy's pose on the billboard inadvertently created phallic opportunities
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed visual comedy with good timing and euphemistic delivery

jokes.text: Great billboard! Funny, edgy, right up to the line without crossing it. Loved it. Which one did you see? Washington street. The one with like 20 dongs on it.
jokes.setup: Creed enthusiastically compliments the billboard
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's praising the vandalized version with 20 dongs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - his oblivious enthusiasm for the wrong thing is character gold

jokes.text: No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No!
jokes.setup: Dwight realizes he was in the billboard photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Extended meltdown with escalating 'No's
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overreaction, but the extended length hurts economy

jokes.text: Schrute farms is very easy to find. It's right in the middle of the root-vegetable district. If the soil starts to get acidic, you've probably gone too far.
jokes.setup: People ask for normal directions
jokes.punchline: Dwight gives agricultural soil-based directions
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight - his farmer logic applied to simple directions is perfectly in character

jokes.text: What happens when we're done chewing? Do we spit it out on the floor or keep it in our mouths the rest of the time?
jokes.setup: Andy gives basic table manner instructions
jokes.punchline: Darryl sarcastically asks about the obvious next step
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic pushback highlighting Andy's condescension

jokes.text: This is not a picnic, Phyllis. It's a garden party. There's a grill in the warehouse I could bring. Ugh, please don't. Not a barbecue. It's better than a barbecue. It's dignified, quieter, there are rules. How is that better than a barbecue?
jokes.setup: Andy insists on garden party superiority
jokes.punchline: His reasons (dignified, quiet, rules) sound worse than fun
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's misguided priorities and pretension well

jokes.text: Connecticut casual. Any chance Connecticut casual is Pennsylvania business? I.E., this is what I'm wearing to your party.
jokes.setup: Andy announces pretentious dress code
jokes.punchline: Stanley deadpans that he's wearing his current clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley - practical and cutting through Andy's pretension

jokes.text: Yeah, whoa, who's the sausage?
jokes.setup: Andy shows video of his family singing
jokes.punchline: Kevin inappropriately calls Andy's dad 'the sausage'
jokes.explanation: Kevin's inappropriate comment is in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I cannot believe that Andy is throwing a party like this just to impress the CEO. Classic Gabe move. Hey, Andy, how about you don't steal my business strategies and I won't dress like my life is just one long brunch?
jokes.setup: Gabe criticizes Andy for sucking up
jokes.punchline: His comeback insult about Andy's brunch-like lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Great insult that captures both characters - Gabe's jealousy and Andy's lifestyle

jokes.text: They don't warn you when the cameras are driving by.
jokes.setup: Looking at street view of Schrute Farms online
jokes.punchline: Dwight is visible in the Google Street View, implying awkward capture
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag with Dwight's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: I'm actually really disappointed in how poorly my book is doing. I've only sold one copy.
jokes.setup: Dwight brags about having the only copy of a rare book
jokes.punchline: Cut to the author lamenting only selling one copy
jokes.explanation: Clever cut revealing the irony of Dwight's 'rare' book purchase

jokes.text: Philip? Philip is the name that we're using. It's after my favorite cat. It's after my grandfather. It's after my cat. It's after my grandfather.
jokes.setup: Both reveal they're naming their babies Philip
jokes.punchline: Awkward back-and-forth over the naming source
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing both characters' defensiveness

jokes.text: One, a pot of marmalade. Oh, that sounds great. Well that-- that is what you want then, the marmalade? Sure. You don't want to hear the other one? You love marmalade? I'll hear the other one. It's a basil plant!
jokes.setup: Mother offers gift options
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-analysis of Andy's preferences
jokes.explanation: Shows family dysfunction but drags without strong payoff

jokes.text: I'm the valet. You have to give me your car. You know what, I can probably park it myself. You have to give me your car. It's probably okay that I park it myself. No, I think it's probably better that I-- I'm the valet. You have to give me your car. Get out of your car. Get out.
jokes.setup: Dwight insists on being valet
jokes.punchline: Increasingly aggressive insistence culminating in commands
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight power trip but could be tighter

jokes.text: Have a good time at the...Thing. It's a little tricky. You might want to be careful-- [CRASH]
jokes.setup: Guest tries to warn Dwight about the car
jokes.punchline: Immediate crash sound as Dwight drives away
jokes.explanation: Well-timed physical comedy with good setup and immediate payoff

jokes.text: Mr. Ryan Howard! Chapter two, announcing guests as they enter is height of decorum. The more volume displayed, the more honor is bestowed upon everyone present. Stanley Hudson, and his mistress Cynthia!
jokes.setup: Dwight announces guests formally per his book
jokes.punchline: Loudly announces Stanley's affair to everyone
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight moment - following rules while creating maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: James, Pamela, and... P.P. Halpert!
jokes.setup: Dwight formally announces Jim and Pam
jokes.punchline: Calls baby Cece 'P.P. Halpert' (pee-pee)
jokes.explanation: Decent Dwight mispronunciation creating unintended bathroom humor

jokes.text: Mabel, mabel, if you're able, keep your elbows off the table.
jokes.setup: Andy hovers annoyingly over guests
jokes.punchline: Recites childish table manner rhyme
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's awkwardness but rhyme itself is basic

jokes.text: Well... Technically for Andy. Technically for Robert California. He's our CEO. I thought you were the CEO. I don't know how you got there. You said you were running the company. This branch. I'm the regional manager. Oh, that makes more sense.
jokes.setup: Andy's parents ask about work hierarchy
jokes.punchline: Reveals Andy has been overselling his importance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment exposing Andy's insecurity and exaggeration

jokes.text: Did I throw this party to impress my parents? That's crazy. Now, if they wanted a garden party, they could throw one themselves. Which, as a matter of fact, they did last week. They threw one for my baby brother. And it was totally amazing, but I couldn't care less.
jokes.setup: Andy denies throwing party to impress parents
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals detailed knowledge of brother's superior party
jokes.explanation: Good talking head showing Andy's self-deception and jealousy

jokes.text: What are you going to see? Moneyball. Walter Jr.'s choice.
jokes.setup: Parents mention having theater tickets
jokes.punchline: They're seeing a baseball movie, not theater
jokes.explanation: Mild subversion of expectations but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Tuna! Right? And this must be your lovely wife, Pam. No. Hell no. She wishes. No, no, no, no, no. No.
jokes.setup: Walter Jr. assumes Jim and Pam are married
jokes.punchline: Multiple increasingly emphatic denials from different people
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward misunderstanding with escalating denials

jokes.text: Chapter four, one of the host's most important duties is as dance master. A proper courtly dance sets the tone for the entire afternoon.
jokes.setup: Dwight reads from his etiquette book
jokes.punchline: Prepares to lead 'courtly dance' at casual garden party
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dwight's formal dance at informal party

jokes.text: R-r-Robert Califor-r-r-rnia!
jokes.setup: Dwight announces Robert California's arrival
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic rolling R's and elongated announcement
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight over-the-top formality

jokes.text: Oh, I thought you were going to bring the marmalade. No, I'm certain you said, 'basil.'
jokes.setup: Mother brings basil instead of marmalade
jokes.punchline: Continues the gift confusion from earlier
jokes.explanation: Callback works but original bit wasn't strong enough to enhance

jokes.text: This is not funny, man. Let me down. I think this thing is broken. It's not broken, Kevin. This is how it works.
jokes.setup: Kevin is somehow stuck or elevated
jokes.punchline: Dwight matter-of-factly explains it's working as intended
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Dwight's typical blunt explanation

jokes.text: Toasts are great. I mean, you toast somebody, they toast you back. It just goes round and round. That's my favorite part about toasts. The reciprocity.
jokes.setup: Andy wants to make a toast
jokes.punchline: Over-explains the obvious concept of reciprocal toasting
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his awkward social analysis

jokes.text: I think we should be acknowledging our boss because none of us would be here without him. Robert California. To Robert! Yay, Robert!
jokes.setup: Jim starts what seems like toast to Andy
jokes.punchline: Pivots to toasting the actual boss, Robert California
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim misdirection that undermines Andy perfectly

jokes.text: I cannot believe I didn't think of toasting Robert. Get in the game, Gabriel.
jokes.setup: Everyone toasts Robert instead of Andy
jokes.punchline: Gabe criticizes himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe character moment with his weird self-talk

jokes.text: I would like to toast someone who isn't here, but who will be in just four short months. Welcome to the world, Philip lipton! I also would like to toast Philip Halpert... Who's due even sooner.
jokes.setup: Angela toasts her unborn baby Philip
jokes.punchline: Pam immediately one-ups with her own Philip
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of earlier Philip conflict in public setting

jokes.text: May he be a good namesake to my grandfather, who I promised as a child, long before tonight, that I would one day name my son after. She just always has to copy anything I do! It's the Ford Taurus situation all over again.
jokes.setup: Pam emphasizes her prior claim to the name
jokes.punchline: Angela references previous copying incident with cars
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Angela's long-held grudges

jokes.text: I'd like to make a toast to the troops. Here here. Yes. All the troops. Both sides.
jokes.setup: Ryan makes patriotic troops toast
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means both sides of conflicts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - fake woke statement that's actually absurd

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, the last supper. Chapter nine, the tableau vivant is not only welcome, but expected entertainment at any garden party.
jokes.setup: Dwight announces tableau vivant entertainment
jokes.punchline: Presents 'Last Supper' tableau at garden party
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dwight moment - religious tableau at casual party

jokes.text: You people say I led you. But it wasn't me. You want to toast the man who led you to success. But the boss is irrelevant. Andy and I produce nothing. We do nothing. We sit in our offices and demand, 'I want this and that, right now,' like petulant children.
jokes.setup: Robert starts what seems like a humble speech
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of management uselessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Robert California - darkly philosophical and brutally honest

jokes.text: You know the difference between a crying baby and a manager? One day, the baby will grow up.
jokes.setup: Sets up comparison between baby and manager
jokes.punchline: Manager will never mature like baby will
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke structure with perfect economy and devastating punchline

jokes.text: But without you, Andy and I would be sitting in our dirty diapers, waiting for someone to change us, wipe us. I should be toasting you, thanking you for allowing me to have the easiest job in the universe.
jokes.setup: Continues baby/manager metaphor
jokes.punchline: Graphic diaper imagery applied to executives
jokes.explanation: Good extension of previous joke but slightly less tight

jokes.text: ♪ Saying I love you, is not the words-- ♪ It's too high. Yeah? Right? It's high, but it's not that high. Right, it feels strange. Just take it down. ♪ Saying I love-- ♪ ♪ saying I love you-- ♪ You know, just give it-- Just give it to me. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to duet with his father
jokes.punchline: Awkward musical negotiation and key changes
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but musical fumbling drags slightly

jokes.text: Is anyone filming this? Seriously? Erin! Yeah, it's either taping or calling.
jokes.setup: Andy desperately wants performance recorded
jokes.punchline: Erin admits she doesn't know if phone is recording
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's technological confusion

jokes.text: Get up there and sing or I will cut your larynx and you'll never be able to sing again.
jokes.setup: Walter Jr. resists joining the performance
jokes.punchline: Father makes violent threat about singing ability
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny threat that reveals family dysfunction

jokes.text: What are you doing there, buddy? What's going on? Cheers, cheers, cheers. Get your own guitar.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to join the successful performance
jokes.punchline: Father dismissively tells him to get his own guitar
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Andy's exclusion from family success

jokes.text: I am so cold. That's because you didn't bring a jacket.
jokes.setup: Mother complains about being cold
jokes.punchline: Father states obvious reason without sympathy
jokes.explanation: Shows parental dysfunction but joke itself is basic

jokes.text: My first love is beet farming, but it's a young man's game. Whoever heard of an old beet farmer?
jokes.setup: Dwight discusses career changes
jokes.punchline: Treats beet farming like professional sports requiring youth
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight absurdism treating farming like athletic career

jokes.text: The pewter package has the least amount of goats. It's not no goats. Still 10 to 12 goats depending on the availability of the goats. Now, the goat package obviously has the most goats.
jokes.setup: Dwight describes party packages
jokes.punchline: All packages involve many goats, even the 'least goats' option
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where the 'least goats' option is still absurd

jokes.text: Of course I am not interested in goats. Why would you spend so much time going over the goats with me? I can get you exotic meats. Hippo steaks, giraffe burgers. We'll talk. It'll all be goat.
jokes.setup: Robert rejects goats, Dwight offers exotic alternatives
jokes.punchline: Dwight admits it'll all be goat anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline revealing Dwight's limitations despite grand promises

jokes.text: Hello, Cece Halpert? This is Andrew Bernard. I'd very much like to speak with you about your paper supply needs. That wasn't even my worst sales call.
jokes.setup: Andy makes sales call to a baby
jokes.punchline: Admits this isn't his worst sales performance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with perfect understated punchline

jokes.text: I'm not going to tell you how impressed I am that you're a manager of some rinky-dink branch of a paper company in Scranton, Pennsylvania.
jokes.setup: Andy wants parental approval
jokes.punchline: Father brutally dismisses Andy's achievements
jokes.explanation: Cutting parental criticism that explains Andy's neediness

jokes.text: I think if I had parents like that, I'd be trying to convince everyone all the time how great I was too. Guess we found Andy's rosebud. Rosebud? It's a reference to citizen Kane.
jokes.setup: Jim analyzes Andy's behavior
jokes.punchline: References Citizen Kane to explain Andy's psychology
jokes.explanation: Good character analysis with cultural reference

jokes.text: I know citizen Kane. Rosebud didn't explain why he was how he was. It just represented what was important to him as a child that he missed. That school of thought-- Let's just agree to disagree. No. You're wrong. Are you sure? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Oscar corrects Jim's Citizen Kane interpretation
jokes.punchline: Jim stubbornly refuses to accept being wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Oscar's know-it-all nature vs Jim's pride

jokes.text: My body has somehow become acclimated to Southern Italy. Isn't that strange? I've never been there.
jokes.setup: Robert explains feeling hot
jokes.punchline: Claims Italian climate acclimation without visiting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Robert California absurdism - philosophical yet nonsensical

jokes.text: Bye, tuna! Bye, Pam!
jokes.setup: Walter Jr. says goodbye
jokes.punchline: Uses Jim's nickname showing he remembers/likes the office dynamic
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that works for character consistency

jokes.text: Cheeseburger or hamburger? Um, cheeseburger? Nard-dog.
jokes.setup: Andy asks Oscar food preference question
jokes.punchline: Uses answer to deliver nickname like a game show
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy finding small ways to connect after party disaster

jokes.text: I think I left my wallet in your house. Who cares? Right here. Mr. James Halpert! Keys, forgot my keys. Stop forgetting things. I didn't forget them. They're right here. That was stupid. Mr. James Halpert! So sorry. I forgot that thing. Idiot. Mi-- what are you doing?
jokes.setup: People keep returning for forgotten items
jokes.punchline: Dwight continues announcing Jim despite the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Dwight's commitment to his role

jokes.text: Who do you think is really the best salesman in this office? That's a stupid question. Obviously... Mr. James Halpert!
jokes.setup: Dwight asks Jim about best salesman
jokes.punchline: Announces Jim as answer while still in valet character
jokes.explanation: Good ending showing Dwight's commitment and Jim recognition

tmdb_overview: Andy plants the seeds of trying to impress Robert California by throwing a garden party at Schrute Farms. Andy's parents and brother also attend the event, only to make him feel awkward.
